[WHAT]
We're building a health track that will be an intense week of learning and testing cutting-edge health ideas and projects. These funds will help key participants joining and support experiments in the health space coming to life.

[IMPACT]
Cities are making people sick and urban environments have more unhealthy people than ever before. Your environment determines your health, and we want to inspire people to build healthier environments and have people test how to actually live in them

[PROGRESS]
This will be the 3rd year of the Edge Esmeralda health track. Edge City has created over 8 pop-up villages that have health as a top 4 priority and we've helped impact over 11,000 lives with the health by default philosophy.

[WHY]
I've helped run this track every year.
what_are_you_making: Health-focused experimental track at Edge Esmeralda
impact: Promotes healthier environments and lifestyle experimentation
why_you: Organizer with experience running the track

[WHAT]
I stress-test narratives in Web3: this funding makes the work a public good (not for lazy startups).

(a) Samaritans: flag a project whose claims don't add up.
(b) Commons: free narrative audit for public goods projects.

All audits to be public.

[IMPACT]
(a) Protect users from projects that look professional but don't hold up: claims that don't match evidence, credentials that don't verify, traction that doesn't exist.

(b) Help public goods projects communicate so their work gets used and funded.

[PROGRESS]
Narrative audits delivered for Blockchain foundations, DeFi protocols, tech infra.

Methodology developed through CLEA research and proven through industry work. 2x hackathon winner in 2025 (Web3 Summit Berlin, Funding the Commons).

Ready to scale.

[WHY]
Years ghostwriting for Web3 protocols: whitepapers, manifestos, marketing strategy. I know what to spot because I've built the narratives from the inside.

Affiliated researcher at CLEA Cybernetic Lab. 
Storyteller for 10 years across industries.
impact: Protect users from misleading projects and help public goods communicate better
progress: Delivered audits for blockchain foundations and DeFi protocols; hackathon wins

raw_text:
[WHAT]
We are building a methodology that reads funding applications and helps decide who should get money for public good projects, instead of paying many humans to do it.

[IMPACT]
By cutting evaluation costs in public goods funding rounds, more money goes directly to climate, community, and social impact projects, helping regenerate ecosystems and support real-world change.

[PROGRESS]
We designed the full research plan, selected five LLM models, defined RBTS vs averaging tests, mapped token costs, identified 5 past rounds to study, and assembled an AI and PGF-focused team.

[WHY]
Our team combines 20+ years in IT, AI engineering expertise, blockchain and PGF experience. This research will lead us to seek consultations from human funding round evaluators to get an insight into their evaluation system.
what_are_you_making: LLM-based evaluation system for funding proposals

[WHAT]
To lower the barrier to entry into DeSci. We unite existing platforms and projects into one accessible hub. No more scattered resources or wondering where to start. Everything you need is collated, collaborative and community-driven.

[IMPACT]
DeSci Bridge helps researchers, students and builders worldwide access knowledge, collaborate across borders and actively contribute to decentralized science.

[PROGRESS]
We just launched and we have partnered with AuraSci. We have curated DeSci educational series as the recommended starter course on DeSci Bridge to onboard researchers, students and builders  into the DeSci ecosystem

[WHY]
As a biomedical engineer and AI researcher, I understand how research actually works. As a community builder, content developer and contributor to DeSci protocols, I know how to onboard people and turn learning into participation.

[WHAT]
A prototype for the future of artistic publishing, where science and technology serves culture, and where art actively shapes how knowledge is produced, owned, and shared.

[IMPACT]
By bringing together art, nature, technology & science from local perspectives, valuing indigenous, non-Western knowledge, challenging dominant global narratives, and helping communities tell their own stories, fairly, ethically, on their own terms.

[PROGRESS]
ANTS is part of a larger initiative called Meta Gallery, a technology-based art space launched in Rio. We have validated the curatorial vision, tested tech driven exhibition models & built an active network of artists, researchers, and institutions.

[WHY]
I bring over two decades of experience across contemporary art, digital culture, and curatorship. I have led exhibitions & institutions that connect art, technology, and critical theory bridging concept to reality.

[WHAT]
We are creating joyful ocean festivals and cultural gatherings that blend music, art, science, and storytelling. Through Web3, we are beginning to translate these experiences into digital collectibles and on-chain cultural records that preserve ocean

[IMPACT]
Mussa helps people reconnect with the ocean through culture and shared experiences. By gradually integrating blockchain tools, we aim to make ocean stories, artistic expressions, and community participation more transparent, traceable, and collective

[PROGRESS]
We have produced 11 ocean cultural events, reaching 1,000+ people. Our first anniversary featured 80+ artists, building a community around ocean art, music, science, and coral reefs, and laying the groundwork for future on-chain cultural records

[WHY]
We are marine scientists and cultural producers with 20+ years of experience in coral reef conservation and event production, working at the intersection of science, art, and community while exploring Web3 for cultural memory and collective impact

[WHAT]
We are building a blockchain-based Data Lake with evidence from Tityus serrulatus venom. A predictive AI Matrix will analyze this data to identify biopharmaceuticals and bio-inputs, accelerating discoveries via open validation all compiled scientifi

[IMPACT]
We are converting a health problem (scorpion stings) into a solution. We democratized bioprospecting via DeSci, reducing drug costs and creating sustainable and regenerate agricultural ecosystems. It's not just a laboratory, it's a protocol coletive.

[PROGRESS]
We defined the Predictive AI architecture and the design of Bio-Oracles for data validation. We consolidated the theoretical basis Deep Tech Telsons and the DAO governance model to ensure ethics and compliance in biodiversity exploration in IA-ML-DL.

[WHY]
We bring together data scientists, biotechnologists, and Web3 experts. We have the technical "viability" and ethical vision to transform Brazilian biodiversity into open science digital assets, overcoming the barriers of "Big Pharma" Open Bio Oracle.

[WHAT]
We’re studying special African plants that people boost energy and vitality. We test them in the lab like a science detective checking what’s inside, what they do, and if they’re safe. We’ll share the ‘how-to’ so others can confirm it

[IMPACT]
The African Scientist Hub is turning African botanicals into trusted, measurable science. By validating S. jollyanum and E. longifolia and releasing open datasets + reproducible methods, we can accelerate better men’s health tools andcreate  value.

[PROGRESS]
Framework + SOPs drafted, with deck + manuscript ready. Presented in DeSci spaces, began partnerships, and opened talks with Bio Protocol, Molecule, and VitaDAO while building African Scientist Hub which is the research pipeline.

[WHY]
Biomedical Scientist with hands-on diagnostics and botanical research experience, building The African Scientist Hub to turn African bioactives into trusted, reproducible science. I blend lab rigor with DeSci tools to make the work auditable

[WHAT]
We are powering little solar mini-grids for remote villages. They catch sunshine and turn it into electricity so homes, schools, and shops can have lights and power every day.

[IMPACT]
Solar Village brings clean power to communities without electricity, helping kids study, businesses grow. We reduce pollution and reliance on charcoal stoves and diesel generators, all while building stronger, self-reliant local economies.

[PROGRESS]
We’ve partnered with UNDP, local governments and a few mini-grid operators. We have designed our energy data platform, selected 23 pilot sites in Nigeria, and are working with global partners on transparent reporting and carbon credit readiness.

[WHY]
We have a strong team that combine energy systems knowledge, local partnerships, and mission-driven leadership, with hands-on experience building real infrastructure that puts communities first, not extractive energy models.

[WHAT]

[IMPACT]

[PROGRESS]

I'm funding a community event for DeSci project leads to do community outreach and using the event survey results to generate a report on current DeSci ecosystem needs for growth and publishing it in open-access on Paragraph.

[IMPACT]
My project will generate knowledge to inform grant funding agencies what the people need to (1) onboard new participants and (2) gain valuable skills and experiences to participate in the DeSci ecosystem in a meaningful way.

[PROGRESS]
I have published 4 articles on Paragraph (https://paragraph.com/@bitcoinuniversity) and have 2 more lined up. Here's a post on the currect platform growth progress (https://x.com/wAliceChen/status/1999137102127063372).

[WHY]
I already created the budget breakdown (https://tinyurl.com/meet-desci-in-asia), designed the surveys, and connected with DeSci's founders.

[WHAT]
FunDeSci is building a decentralized NFT marketplace and NFT- Fundraising where researchers can tokenize proposals and research assets, to attract funding and trade outputs, making science more accessible, fundable, and community-driven.

[IMPACT]
FunDeSci will democratize access to scientific research funding, empowering researchers worldwide, especially those in underfunded or marginalized regions to pursue innovative ideas without relying on traditional gatekeepers

[PROGRESS]
Beta Version, Currently, FunDeSci supports four leading blockchain networks, Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Binance, and Optimism for two fundraising methods, including Crowdfunding and Auction. We have strong collaboration with DeSci Asia, Hippocrat

[WHY]
We directly tackle the challenge of limited scientific funding, Our NFT-based approach is revolutionizing the research funding landscape, We have a passionate and experienced team with proven expertise in blockchain, finance, and scientific research

[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
DeSci Asia: Building bridges, sharing knowledge and fostering growth for DeSci communities in Asia.
 
 We have a diverse and active DeSci Asia telegram group with over 400 members from different places such as Thailand, India, China, Japan, Indonesia, Hong Kong, Taiwan and more.
 
 https://t.me/DeSciAsia
 
 DeSci Asia is a collaborative community engaging with academics, nonprofit organizations, and researchers focused on decentralized science and ecological conservation.

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
Our goals for 2025 are:
 
 To apply for Gitcoin and other public goods grants to increase our visibility and attract more DeSci enthusiasts. All the funds raised will be used to support the following activities:
 
 50% of the funds will go to DeSci education
 
 30% of the funds will go to local DeSci meetups and events
 
 20% of the funds will go to DeSci Asia long term community development and sustainability

raw_text:
[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
Problem Statement
 Scientific research is often hindered by traditional funding barriers, including lengthy application processes, centralized grant distribution, and biases in funding allocation. Many innovative research projects?specially from early-career researchers, independent scientists, and interdisciplinary fields?truggle to secure funding through conventional means. Additionally, intellectual property (IP) protection, transparency, and commercialization remain key challenges in scientific funding.
 
 Solution
 FunDeSci is a multi-chain decentralized crowdfunding platform designed to revolutionize scientific funding by leveraging blockchain and NFTs. Our platform enables researchers to:
 
 Tokenize research ideas, datasets, algorithms, and technical reports as NFTs for funding and IP protection.
 
 Launch crowdfunding campaigns using Polygon, Arbitrum, Binance, and Optimism, accepting both native and stablecoins.
 
 Engage with a decentralized funding community including individual backers, DAOs, and institutional supporters.
 
 Ensure transparency and accountability using blockchain technology for fund tracking and milestone-based disbursement.
 
 Bridge academia and industry, allowing research projects to transition into commercial ventures.
 
 Roadmap & Timeline
 ??Phase 1 ??Platform Development & Beta Launch (Q1 2024 - Q2 2024)
 
 Developed FunDeSci? core crowdfunding functionalities.
 
 Integrated multi-chain support (Polygon, Arbitrum, Binance, Optimism).
 
 Launched a beta version with early adopters.
 
 ?? Phase 2 ??Community Growth & Strategic Partnerships (Q2 2024 - Q3 2024)
 
 Built a network of 370+ researchers on LinkedIn, 280+ Telegram members, and 230+ Twitter followers.
 
 Established partnerships with DeSci Asia, DeSci Tokyo, DeSci Sino, and DeSci India.
 
 Successfully launched the Asian-Based Grant for DeSci & DAOs.
 
 ? Phase 3 ??Expanding Adoption & Grant Programs (Q3 2024 - Q4 2024)
 
 Strengthen collaborations with academic institutions, DeSci communities, and research DAOs.
 
 Launch additional grant rounds to fund innovative projects.
 
 Enhance platform functionalities for milestone-based funding disbursement.
 
 ?? Phase 4 ??Full-Scale Launch & Web3 Integration (2025 & Beyond)
 
 Expand FunDeSci? reach to global research institutions and independent scientists.
 
 Implement decentralized governance (DAO) for funding allocation.
 
 Develop new DeFi mechanisms to provide sustainable funding streams for research.
 
 FunDeSci is on a mission to democratize scientific funding, ensuring every great idea gets the chance it deserves.

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
FunDeSci democratizes scientific funding by leveraging blockchain, NFTs, and DAOs, ensuring transparent, decentralized, and community-driven support for researchers.
 
 _ Decentralized Crowdfunding: Bypasses institutional barriers, allowing researchers to raise funds directly.
 
 -NFT-Based Asset Protection: Tokenizes research outputs (datasets, algorithms, reports) to secure ownership and commercialization.
 
 -Transparent & Accountable Funding: Smart contracts ensure milestone-based fund disbursement, increasing trust.
 
 - Bridging Academia & Industry: Connects researchers with industry partners for real-world applications.
 
 - Strengthening the Global DeSci Ecosystem: Partners with DeSci Asia, DeSci Tokyo, DeSci Sino, and DeSci India to expand decentralized research funding.

[USE OF FUNDS]
The funds from the grants program will be used to launch and scale the second Asian Grant for DeSci, fostering decentralized scientific research and innovation across Asia. Specifically, the funds will support:
 
 - Grant Pool for Researchers ??Direct funding for top-tier DeSci projects, ensuring groundbreaking research gets financial backing.
 - Operational & Platform Enhancements ??Improving FunDeSci? infrastructure to streamline the application, review, and funding process.
 - Community & Outreach ??Expanding engagement with researchers, DAOs, and institutions across Asia to increase participation.
 - Marketing & Awareness ??Promoting the grant to attract high-quality proposals and funding partners.
 - Review & Selection Process ??Ensuring fair and transparent evaluation through a decentralized selection committee.
 
 This initiative will strengthen Asia? role in the DeSci ecosystem, providing a sustainable funding model for decentralized research.

[PAST ROUND WALLET]
The same wallet for GG21 DeSci round

[NEW UPDATES]
Milestones are mentioned on KarmaGap
 
 https://gap.karmahq.xyz/project/fundesci-fundraising-platform-for-desci?tab=updates

raw_text:
[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
Scientific brain drain continues to be a major challenge in many regions, particularly in the Global South. Talented young scientists often leave their home countries in search of better opportunities, leading to a loss of valuable expertise that could have been harnessed for local development. Additionally, the rise of the attention economy has made it difficult for young people to engage deeply with scientific research, as they are constantly exposed to fast-paced, surface-level content that does not encourage critical thinking or long-term academic involvement. Without a clear pathway for engagement, young researchers miss out on opportunities to contribute meaningfully to scientific progress, both locally and globally.
 
 Solution
 DeSci Youths - Dewellpunk Chapter is a platform designed to nurture and empower the next generation of citizen scientists by connecting them with Decentralized Science (DeSci) initiatives. Our approach is threefold:
 
 Showcasing DeSci Projects: We will curate and highlight various DeSci initiatives to educate students on how web3 technologies can revolutionize scientific collaboration and funding.
 
 Onboarding Space: Through interactive workshops, online resources, and mentorship programs, we will provide students with a structured way to explore and participate in DeSci.
 
 Campus Tours in African Universities: By visiting campuses and hosting talks, hackathons, and networking events, we will introduce students to DeSci and create pathways for them to engage with decentralized scientific research and funding models.
 
 Our roadmap and timeline is categorized by 4 phases and we are still on our first 2 phases namely, 
 Phase 1: Research & Community Building (Months 1-9)
 Phase 2: Content Development & Awareness Campaign (Months 7-12)
 Our last phases breakout will precede later on namely,
  Phase 3: Campus Tours & Onboarding 
 Phase 4: Scaling & Sustained Engagement

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
DeSci is about democratizing access to scientific knowledge, funding, and collaboration. Our project directly aligns with this mission by:
 
 Lowering the barriers to entry for young scientists and students to engage with decentralized research initiatives.
 
 Creating awareness and fostering adoption of decentralized funding models for science.
 
 Building a grassroots movement that ensures scientific progress is accessible, community-driven, and beneficial to local societies.

[USE OF FUNDS]
If granted funding, we will allocate resources to support students projects that has started building in DeSci.
 Some of the funds will be used to develop a digital platform that houses our community.
 We are currently doing research and logistics on DeSci campus tours in East Africa region.

[PAST ROUND WALLET]


[NEW UPDATES]
We have consistently been keeping track of DeSci digital footprint by sharing updates and keeping the records at karma gap.
 On our weekly updates through our pinned tag on x we share x spaces , podcasts and events in the line up of the incoming week. 
 We have also participated in both online and IRL web 3 events.
 We onboarded 2 students building in DeSci from our region and we continue to support them

[OTHER INFORMATION]

raw_text:
[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
MesoReefDAO: The Regen Reef initiative blends marine biotech, socio-ecological restoration, and ReFi to create modular wet labs for coral and fish regeneration starting in Mesoamerica.

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
MesoReefDAO aligns with the mission and goals of Decentralized Science (DeSci) by leveraging blockchain technology, open science principles, and decentralized governance to enhance coral reef conservation and marine ecosystem restoration. Here? how:
 
 Open & Transparent Scientific Research
 
 Funding Scientific Innovation
 
 Decentralized Governance & Collaboration
 
 Bridging the Digital Divide in Marine Science

[USE OF FUNDS]
Research & Coral Reef Restoration
 Objective: Scale restoration efforts while fostering scientific collaboration across the Mesoamerican Reef.
 
 a). Partner with 3+ regional stakeholders (e.g., Cozumel Coral Conservatory, Infinita City, among others) to deploy Regen Reef prototypes.
 
 Financial Incentives for Scholars & Volunteers
 Objective: Empower the next generation of marine scientists and local stewards.
 
 a) Develop Regen Reef incentives program using Celo
 
 Administration & Operations
 Objective: Ensure legal/financial sustainability as a Mexican NGO/DAO.
 
 a) Hire a legal advisor to structure hybrid NGO-DAO compliance
 
 b) Accountant fees
 
 Community Governance.
 Objective: Implement a decentralized governance framework that empowers stakeholders to participate in decision-making and fosters active community engagement.
 
 a) Research & Select Voting Tools: Evaluate and choose a Web3 governance platform (e.g., Snapshot, Tally) for proposal creation and voting.
 
 Tech Stack.
 Objective: Upgrade MesoReefDAO? digital infrastructure to a scalable Web3 interface that enhances user experience, security, and interoperability.
 
 a) Website Redesign: Modernize the UI/UX, focusing on accessibility, multilingual support (Spanish/English), and mobile optimization.

raw_text:
[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
Asterisk Women? Health addresses the persistent gap in women? non-reproductive health data which has remained overlooked by the medical field for decades. This issue? stems from the false belief that women are just small men (aka. Bikini Medicine) and was deepened in the 1970s when women of reproductive age were systematically excluded from clinical trials.This resulted in men being used as the baseline for health research and treatments for the majority of Western Medicine.? Such exclusion created an ongoing deficit in female-specific health data, especially outside reproductive health, where? 80% of MedTech funding is concentrated. This underfunding and lack of targeted research have left many aspects of women? health underserved. 
 
 Our aim is to provide researchers with the first female-specific data pools for female-biased disorders while providing women the ability to vote on their healthcare for the first time in history through tokenizing the data. We will do this by building the first female-only data union (aka. data lake or data pool) which collects, aggregates, and monetizes anonymized women? health data while ensuring privacy through zero-knowledge compute methods. Our solution provides researchers cost-effective aquisition of ongoing metrics from regions traditionally left out of the research equation, as well as cost-effective aquisition of focus group and trial participants (should our participants opt in in their profiles).
 
 Our MVP begins alpha testing in April!

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
Medical data ownership is at the fore of the concerns DeSci addresses. Our solution leaves data in the hands of our participants, while lowering the barrier to acquisition for researchers in desperate need of more female-specific data.

[USE OF FUNDS]
All funding from GitCoin will go directly to developing our MVP into a beta app (Q2) and building the customer portal to bring us to monetization in Q3.

[PAST ROUND WALLET]


[NEW UPDATES]
- Won 1st Place Akave Bounty at ETH Bangkok
 - Won 2nd Place Vana Bounty at HER DAO Hackathon
 - Finalist for UNICEF women and girls grant
 - Our founder keynoted Eve Wealth Summit in LA
 - Developing our MVP which will launch to Alpha in April

[OTHER INFORMATION]
We continue to be 90% female-led (our developer is male) and 100% bootstrapped. GitCoin has provided a critical lifeline for our growth. Going to market is now in sight and we appreciate this final push!

[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
Problem statement. Scientific publishing houses and science journals have outrageously high publication fees. These fees range from 2 to 6k USD for 1 paper, some charge 10k-15 USD/ paper. Open access is another 2-4k USD. Currently, it is right to say that the scientific publication industry hinders the advance of human knowledge. 
 Paying attention only to their profits, legacy scientific journals: 
 ??Act as filters to what they believe is high quality research worth publishing;
 ??use high rejection rates which are uncoupled from merit, significance, and quality, 
 ??take a long time from submission to publishing;
 ??undermine the expectations of the scientific community and the development of the human society;
 ??authors (scientists, professionals and, scholars), reviewers and most editors (also scientists) do not get paid for working in the peer-reviewing process of papers for science journals. 
 ??Authors: 1) pay for publishing, 2) pay for open access and moreover, 3) have their copyright expropriated. 
 ??Universities and Research Institutions pay shockingly high annual subscription fees so that alumni and staff have access to journals.
 
 
 Solution. A journal-as-a-service publications platform for collaborative creation of technical and scientific publications, leveraging web3 principles and technologies: 100% copyright to authors, different percentages of authorship (as built-in feature) and scientific assets construction (through blockchain registration). 
 
 
 Roadmap and Timeline. Our plans for Version 4 focus on building a window showcase for registered projects and ideas, laying the foundation for a decentralized marketplace where researchers and startups can monetize scientific knowledge. This version will introduce a new revenue stream, with the platform earning a small fee on each register transaction while fostering transparent and open exchange (6 months). 
 To achieve these goals, the grant will be used to redesign and integrate the registration platform into the publishing platform, ensuring seamless UI and functionality (2 to 3 months). Additionally for version 4, we will scale up marketing efforts, organizing targeted events, workshops, and community activations to raise awareness and onboard new users (3 to 12 months). Through strategic collaborations, we aim to drive adoption and establish our platform as a leading force in decentralized science innovation, ultimately paving the way for a fully operational scientific marketplace in Version 5.

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
The DeSci Journals contribute to the DeSci ecosystem by providing a decentralized, open-access publishing infrastructure that enhances transparency, accessibility, and collaboration. By leveraging blockchain technology, we ensure that research findings, peer reviews, and metadata remain openly accessible and verifiable, fostering trust and reproducibility. Our platform incentivizes knowledge creators, inventors, scientists and researchers through reputation systems and NFT-minted papers. We integrate with open-source repositories like GitHub and decentralized storage solutions, allowing researchers to directly link and publish code alongside their studies for proper citation. This enables permissionless collaboration, allowing global contributors to expand, fork, and improve open-source projects without institutional gatekeeping. Additionally, we embed open-source licenses and ensure long-term data preservation through decentralized storage, protecting contributions from being lost or paywalled. By facilitating transparent and immutable publishing, we help researchers showcase and validate their work more effectively. Our approach not only supports DeSci development but also strengthens the integrity of scientific research through open peer review. Through marketing efforts and community engagement, we promote the adoption of open science principles and decentralized publishing, making scientific knowledge more accessible, verifiable, and community-driven.

[USE OF FUNDS]
Funds will be used to redesign and integrate the registration platform into the publishing platform, ensuring seamless UI and functionality aiming to build a window showcase for registered projects and ideas, laying the foundation for a decentralized marketplace where researchers and startups can monetize scientific knowledge. This version will introduce a new revenue stream, with the platform earning a small fee on each register transaction while fostering transparent and open exchange. Additionally for version 4, we will scale up marketing efforts, organizing targeted events, workshops, and community activations to raise awareness and onboard new users.

[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
Problem Statement
 Integrating precise and verifiable geospatial data within blockchain applications remains a significant challenge. Traditional geospatial services are often centralized, lacking transparency and complete user control, which limits their application in scientific and decentralized environments.
 
 Solution:
 Astral addresses these challenges by developing open-source tools and protocols enabling secure, verifiable, and decentralized sharing of geospatial data. Our solutions include:
  
 Astral Location Proof Protocol: Enables applications to securely verify location data's authenticity.
 
 Astral SDK: This gives developers the tools needed to integrate geospatial capabilities into their decentralized applications effortlessly.
 
 Roadmap and Timeline for Implementation:
 
 Q2 2025: Enhance the Astral SDK for broader developer adoption and integrate additional geospatial data verification protocols.
 
 Q3 2025: Launch the next version of the Location Proof Protocol, which will include improved scalability and privacy features using zero-knowledge proofs.
 
 Q4 2025: Deploy the updated protocols on mainnet and expand our partnerships with academic and research institutions to test and validate our tools in real-world scientific studies.
 
 Q1 2026: Focus on community-driven development and conduct several hackathons and workshops to increase community engagement and feedback.

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
Astral? project supports and aligns with the mission of DeSci. Our focus is on conducting the necessary research, establishing standards, and building the core tooling necessary to foster decentralized geospatial discipline. We believe our work will enable geospatial data collection via geotagged measurements and verifiable climate modeling. Presently, we work directly with researchers at the University of Maryland and various NGOs as a chair for a Decentralized Geospatial Web Working Group (DGWWG).
 Our commitment is to advance the geospatial disciplines and work with an array of builders, researchers, and developers who share our vision and mission.

[USE OF FUNDS]
Grant funding will be allocated to our primary researcher and developer, allowing them to conduct research and open-source software development. Their focus is outlined in our roadmap. The impact of that work is to establish decentralized data science, collection, and applications and develop the necessary tooling to enable location-based applications.

raw_text:
[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
## **Problem** 
 Current scientific funding is broken. Traditional models are plagued by:
 
 * Inefficient, bureaucratic grant processes 
 * Short-term thinking that stifles innovative research 
 * Valuable research locked behind expensive paywalls 
 * Researchers spending more time seeking funding than conducting science
 
 ## **Solution** 
 We're building science funding as a "hyperstructure" ??a crypto-economic protocol that runs forever without maintenance, cannot be stopped, and creates value for all participants.
 
 This means: scientists receive continuous support rather than one-off grants.
 
 CCGF operates on two simple principles:
 
 * **Community-driven selection via conviction voting:** Projects are selected for support based on sustained community conviction. 
 * **Fair distribution:** Resources flow to projects proportionally to their community support, using StreamingQF to balance large and small backers.
 
 When funded projects succeed, they voluntarily contribute back to the ecosystem through revenue sharing, token contributions, and resource exchange. This feedback loop expands the treasury, enabling continuous funding for new research.
 
 ## **Roadmap & timelines** 
 Near-term milestones for the next 3-6 months are planned as follows:
 
 1. Get 10 projects applying for the continuous funding via CCGF. 
 2. Establish robust operations and governance on Gardens. 
 3. Prepare and launch a StreamingQF round.

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
Our project addresses crucial shortcomings of the traditional science funding model, proposing a framework for continuous support for decentralized projects and scientists to establish a sustainable hyperstructure for DeSci.

[USE OF FUNDS]
We will use the funds to support operations within HyperDeSci and initiate the first funding rounds to test the framework, collecting empirical data.

[PAST ROUND WALLET]
n/a

[NEW UPDATES]
Please see our updates on [Karma GAP](https://gap.karmahq.xyz/project/hyperdesci-operations--summoning-megapi).

raw_text:
[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
*Problem Statement*: Legal research is plagued by multiple inefficiencies resulting from siloed annotations. The absence of transparent and reliable incentives for sharing notes results in gridlocked collaboration and unnecessary duplication. On the other hand, the absence of high-quality, ethically sourced, human-annotated datasets impedes the development of reliable, domain-specific language models for the legal field. Current AI models often rely on unethically sourced data (or noisy and synthetic data parsed from documents), leading to inaccuracies and hallucinations, making them unsuitable for legal applications. There is an urgent need for a system that recognizes and rewards the semantic labor of legal researchers while providing high-quality datasets for AI training, evaluation and benchmarking. 
 
 *Solution*: Hyvmind offers an elegant solution to this problem by creating a reward channel which turns siloed legal annotations into community-owned vector databases. 
 
 *Roadmap*: Presently, the project is in the community development phase. The immediate goal is to solidify institutional partnerships (with civil society organisations, law colleges, other legal-tech projects) by positioning Hyvmind as a tamper-proof and community-owned semantic data layer for legal research. 
 
 The concrete goals over the next year are as follows: 
 - July 2025: launch a 'digital internship pilot' with partner organisations 
 - Aug 2025: build an initial cohort of law students (20-50) around a particular working group (say 'Indian banking law) through blockchain-based rewards (e.g. Push Points) and/or poaps
 - Oct 2025: conduct at least 3 workshops and seminars to onboard fresh cohorts and deepen institutional partnerships
 - Nov 2025: make datasets available through public repos for wider consultation and use; another round of internships and onboarding 
 - Dec 2025: launch beta version with a minimal frontend and user profiles (with rewards, reputation points and annotation marketplace)
 - Feb 2026: feedback, review, finalisation of tokenomics
  - May 2026: launch of v1 app for broader public use

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
- *Digital Public Infrastructure*: there will be two layers to Hyvmind, an openly accessible layer for black letter law (no weights), and a royalty-embedded layer for interpretations/notes (weighted) [see 'annotation value chains' in the whitepaper for details]
 - *Ethically Sourced Datasets*: each 'location' (say art. 14 of the Indian Constitution) will be owned and controlled by its own sub-DAO [see 'location contracts' in the whitepaper for details]
 - *Professional Recognition*: rewards, reputations and annotation weights will combine to form a reliable metric for professional contribution (outside traditional metrics such as cases won, publications etc.) 
 - *Scientific Peer-Production*: the open-source layer of black letter law will be thoroughly vetted by peers, embedding peer review and co-production into the architecture. [see the use of 'inter annotator agreements' in the whitepaper for details]
 - *Progressive Decentralisation through subDAOs*: each location will begin with small working groups which will be progressively decentralised through subDAOs.

[USE OF FUNDS]
- Technical Development (app interface, open-source repos, annotation marketplace, reputation system)
 - Community Growth (workshops, internship pilots, content creation)
 - Research and Development (DAO structuring, tokenomics, royalty parameters, stakeholder discussions)

[PAST ROUND WALLET]


[NEW UPDATES]
not applicable

[OTHER INFORMATION]
For full disclosure: I am currently part of the DeSci Asia, Desci India and Gitcoin Desci Round Ops teams (coordinator), and work as a technical content writer with Push Chain.

source_project_name: OpenLitterMap: Real-Time In-World Open Source Gamification System
raw_text:
[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
Inspired by OpenStreetMap and starting with litter & plastic pollution, OpenLitterMap is creating a real-time in-world gamified data collection layer for the internet.
 
 Starting pre-iphone in 2008, we are making the act of collecting data fun and easy. Today, OpenLitterMap has grown into one of the largest, oldest, and most-open real-time geographic databases in the world.
 
 Simply take a photo, tag it and upload it.
 
 Each upload generates a unique geolink that you can share with anyone to identify any observation in real time:
 
 For example, see how members of the Cardano4Climate community used our app to track donation expenditure for humanitarian relief in Malawi:
 
 https://openlittermap.com/global?lat=-15.810519125694224&lon=35.09398790743067&zoom=18.97&photo=396487
 
 Currently the founder and sole developer is working on a major refactor of the code with OLM v5 which will introduce a significantly improved tagging system, new gamification elements, and other major releases.
 
 Follow along here:
 https://github.com/OpenLitterMap/openlittermap-web/pull/668

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
OpenLitterMap empowers anyone to collect data about anything anywhere and share it openly in real time.

[USE OF FUNDS]
Speed up OLM v5
 
 Prepare 500,000 photos for OLM AI

[PAST ROUND WALLET]
GG22 & GG23

[NEW UPDATES]
Significant progress on OLM v5

[OTHER INFORMATION]

raw_text:
[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
## **Problem** 
 While most efforts in the longevity biotechnology industry are directed toward intracellular processes, **overlake.bio** explores the cellular environment, represented by the **extracellular matrix** (ECM). Operating at the intersection of molecular biology, mechanobiology, and biochemistry, the project offers a comprehensive framework for targeting hitherto irreparable forms of age-related damage.
 
 ## **Solution** 
 *De novo* engineering of enzymes with enhanced functionalities and/or custom target specificities opens broad possibilities for achieving cellular resilience, damage repair, and carrying out novel reactions, working towards optimal human health and longevity. The project aims to ameliorate the mechanosensory burden of the aged ECM through computational biology and reaction-conditioned enzyme engineering to promote youthful ECM remodeling. With its scope outside the cell-centric view on aging, the project addresses a critical conceptual and technological gap within modern longevity research, aiming to extend the healthy human lifespan by leveraging the body? intrinsic ability to restore itself, engineering the ECM turnover.

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
**overlake.bio** is an example of a fully decentralized hard science initiative, funded exclusively through web3 mechanisms and independently executed. We work toward establishing DeSci as a valid professional career path for scientists, a viable alternative to academia and industry.

[USE OF FUNDS]
The funds will be used to support **overlake.bio**? publication activities planned for 2025: 
 - Revision and submission to a peer-reviewed journal of the [preprint](https://dx.doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.4566939) describing our findings on the effects of ribosomal glycation on mRNA decoding accuracy. 
 - Finalization and submission to a peer-reviewed journal of a collaborative [review paper](https://doi.org/10.55277/researchhub.rb3ck05l) on popular longevity biomarkers. 
 - Finalization and publication of a preprint entitled (tentatively): **Production of the recombinant human glyoxalase DJ-1/PARK7 in methylotrophic yeast *Pichia pastoris*** 
 - Finalization and publication of a preprint entitled (tentatively): **Bacterial expression and purification of the recombinant human histone H3.1 for probing electrophilic adduction *in vitro***

[PAST ROUND WALLET]


[NEW UPDATES]
Please see our updates on [Karma GAP](https://gap.karmahq.xyz/project/overlake--bio).

raw_text:
[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
Problem
 
 Scientific progress is being strangled by bureaucracy. Researchers waste 116 hours per grant application?ime that should fuel discovery, not paperwork. For a single funding council, the collective time scientists spend writing proposals equals 550 full-time researchers working an entire year, despite success rates below 20%. Studies confirm the bitter truth: time invested in grant writing doesn't even improve funding outcomes. The result? Promising research dies on the vine not because it lacks merit, but because our broken system rewards administrative skill over scientific brilliance?ystematically suffocating innovation before it can breathe.
 
 Solution
 
 Ideosphere's prediction markets transform scientific speculation into direct research funding, with researchers receiving cash flow through trading activity spread on their prediction markets. Through our robust two-stage validation process?ndependent verification of milestones with UMA-powered dispute resolution?e ensure integrity while creating powerful incentives: researchers receive continuous funding as market activity grows, while science enthusiasts monetize their expertise by backing promising research early. This revolutionary model means researchers earn more as interest in their work increases, creating a virtuous cycle where scientific progress and financial rewards align perfectly.
 
 Roadmap
 
 Phase 1: Foundation (Q2-Q3 2025)
 
 - Complete MVP development with core prediction market functionality
 - Onboard 3-5 pilot researchers with focused, verifiable hypotheses in supplement efficacy
 - Build initial community of science-oriented traders through targeted outreach
 - Launch beta version with limited markets to test mechanisms and gather feedback
 
 Phase 2: Validation & Expansion (Q4 2025-Q1 2026)
 
 - Refine platform based on beta feedback and optimize user experience
 - Expand researcher onboarding process with streamlined verification procedures
 - Develop partnerships with 1-2 research institutions for credibility and pipe
 
 Phase 3: Growth & Ecosystem Development (Q2-Q4 2026)
 
 - Scale researcher onboarding with verified track record and results
 - Implement community governance for market creation and dispute resolution
 - Develop reputation systems for both researchers and traders

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
Ideosphere embodies DeSci's core mission by creating a decentralized funding mechanism that bypasses traditional gatekeepers while aligning scientific progress with market incentives. 
 
 Our prediction markets democratize research by allowing anyone to back promising science and potentially profit from correct outcome predictions. This transforms how research is valued: scientists receive continuous funding based on market interest rather than grant-writing skills; overlooked research finds support through crowd wisdom; and all transactions remain transparent on-chain.

[USE OF FUNDS]
#NAME?

raw_text:
[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
Problem Statement
  The current global governance system, grounded in centralized nation-states and multilateral institutions, has proven increasingly inadequate in addressing the systemic risks and polycrises of the 21st century?rom climate collapse to geopolitical instability and digital sovereignty. Amid this governance failure, new models like Network States and DAOs (Decentralized Autonomous Organizations) have emerged as experimental alternatives, yet their legitimacy, functionality, and scalability remain under-explored, especially in connection to scientific governance and knowledge production.
 Solution
  My project is a transdisciplinary, doctoral-level research initiative that seeks to explore and clarify whether blockchain-enabled Network States can serve as viable models of decentralized global governance. Grounded in global studies and using a decolonial lens, this project investigates how these structures?nabled by smart contracts, decentralized finance, and DAO-based coordination?an reshape how we govern science, identity, territory, and collective action beyond the state.
 The research contributes to the DeSci movement by producing:
 Conceptual clarity on decentralized governance models for science.
 Case study analyses of emergent Network States (e.g., GitcoinDAO, Nation3, Pangea, Praxis, Etc).
 Open-access publication of research tiers via decentralized storage (IPFS/Arweave).
 Interviews with DAO and Network State builders to identify real-world barriers and strategies.
 
 
 Roadmap & Timeline
 Phase 1: Conceptual Framework and Chapter Completion (March?ay 2025)
 Finalization of theoretical chapters: Polycrisis, Blockchain Governance, Network States.
 Integration of key literature (Castells, Srinivasan, Bauwens, Biermann, De Filippi, Ostrom).
 
 
  Phase 2: Fieldwork and Data Collection (April?ay 2025)
 Semi-structured interviews with key figures from Draper Nation, Satoshi Island, Praxis, and Prospera.
 Participant observation during DAO governance events (e.g., Ipe City DAO Brazil).
 
 
  Phase 3: Data Analysis and Empirical Interpretation (June?ugust 2025)
 Thematic coding of interviews.
 Comparative analysis of governance mechanisms, legitimacy, scalability, and community engagement.
 
 
  Phase 4: Synthesis and Dissemination (Sept 2025?arch 2026)
 Drafting and peer review of final chapters.
 Open-source publication of dissertation and research papers on-chain.
 Public workshops or DAO-hosted governance roundtables to discuss findings.
 
 
  April 2026: Final Submission and Public Release
 Submission of dissertation to Humboldt University and USAL.
 On-chain documentation of final insights, milestones, and contributions.

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
My project directly supports the mission of DeSci by advancing the conceptual and empirical understanding of decentralized governance systems that underpin the future of open, transparent, and community-driven science. While many DeSci initiatives focus on specific infrastructure (e.g., funding, publishing, or peer review), this research investigates the governance layer that enables sustainable, legitimate, and scalable coordination across DeSci ecosystems.
 Specifically, it contributes to DeSci? goals in the following ways:
  1. Governance as Public Infrastructure
  By analyzing Network States and DAOs as governance primitives for science, my project helps clarify how decentralized systems can coordinate research funding, knowledge validation, and data stewardship?ore challenges in DeSci. This aligns with DeSci? mission to create bottom-up, community-governed models that go beyond the limitations of academia, venture science, or centralized platforms.
 2. Scientific Legitimacy in Web3 Contexts
  Drawing from the fields of global governance, political economy, and decentralized systems, the project evaluates how DAOs can produce legitimate, inclusive, and trustable mechanisms for conducting and funding science. This helps address a key concern in DeSci: how to build and scale scientific communities without centralized gatekeeping.
 3. Bridging Theory and Practice
  The research is not just theoretical?t engages directly with real-world DeSci actors such as GitcoinDAO, Praxis, and Nation3, offering grounded analysis of their governance strategies. Insights are shared back with the community through open-access publications, conference presentations, and on-chain documentation, thus enriching the DeSci knowledge commons.
  4. Education and Conceptual Innovation
  As a bi-national doctoral project rooted in Global Studies, the work introduces DeSci to broader academic and policy audiences. It brings DeSci into dialogue with global governance theory, platform cooperativism, and digital sovereignty debates?upporting the educational mission of the movement to redefine science as a planetary public good.

[USE OF FUNDS]
Funds from the Gitcoin Grants program will directly support the empirical phase of my doctoral research on Network States and decentralized governance models. Specifically, they will be used to:
 Fund travel to Ipe City (Brazil) in May 2025, a major gathering for decentralized governance and Web3 communities, where I will conduct fieldwork and participant observation with key actors in the DeSci and Network State ecosystem.
 
 
 Support interviews with Network State founders and DAO governance coordinators, including GitcoinDAO participants, to document best practices, challenges, and governance innovations relevant to DeSci.
 
 
 Cover costs related to recording, transcription, and qualitative analysis tools for processing interview data and producing open-access research outputs.
 
 
 Enable participation in community-led events, workshops, and DAO forums to bridge academic research with live decentralized governance experimentation.
 
 
 This funding will ensure that the project is grounded in real-world interactions, not just theory, and that findings are co-produced with the communities driving decentralized science forward.

[PAST ROUND WALLET]
https://beta.checker.gitcoin.co/view/application/%20%20%20%2042220/16/34
 
 https://beta.checker.gitcoin.co/view/application/%20%20%20%2042220/18/4

[NEW UPDATES]
-

[OTHER INFORMATION]
At this time, I have no additional information to include. There are no conflicts of interest or external affiliations that would compromise the integrity of this project. All relevant details about my research, affiliations, and objectives have been provided in the application.

raw_text:
[PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
The Register Maxi project is a pioneering platform designed to address the challenges of intellectual property protection, document validation, and innovation registration in the digital age. In a world where intellectual assets and digital creations are at the heart of innovation, traditional registration methods have often been slow, expensive, and prone to fraud. Furthermore, creators face the risk of losing ownership or recognition of their ideas due to inadequate protection. The problem lies in the inefficiencies of current systems and the growing need for a more secure, transparent, and accessible way to protect and validate digital content across the globe.
 
 Solution
 
 Register Maxi offers a decentralized solution for intellectual property protection through blockchain technology. By leveraging blockchain? immutability and transparency, Register Maxi provides a reliable platform for registering documents, inventions, certifications, contracts, and other digital assets. Instead of storing the full content of the document, the platform only saves encrypted metadata, ensuring privacy while offering verifiable proof of ownership and originality. With blockchain records that are auditable and legally valid under international agreements like the Berne Convention, creators can secure their intellectual property in a matter of minutes, significantly reducing the time and cost compared to traditional processes. The platform is designed to be accessible worldwide, with its decentralized approach providing greater security and reducing the potential for disputes or infringements.
 
 Roadmap & Timeline
 
 The Register Maxi project has a clear roadmap for growth and expansion. The first phase involves the launch of the platform? core features, including the ability to register digital documents, verify authenticity, and integrate blockchain-based smart contracts for managing intellectual property. This phase is expected to be completed in Q2 2025, with initial rollouts in key markets.
 
 In the second phase, Register Maxi will expand its functionality to include integration with various industry-specific platforms, such as startups, universities, and research institutions. This will enhance the platform's reach and make it a go-to solution for academic research and business applications. This phase is projected for completion in Q4 2025.
 
 The final phase will focus on further improving the platform? user interface, expanding to additional regions, and integrating with decentralized networks and Web3 technologies. The anticipated timeline for full-scale deployment and global adoption is set for mid-2026, by which time Register Maxi aims to be a leader in decentralized intellectual property registration, offering comprehensive solutions for creators, researchers, and businesses globally.
 
 By offering a scalable, secure, and legally sound method for intellectual property protection, Register Maxi is poised to transform the way digital creators and innovators safeguard their work in an increasingly digital and interconnected world.

[DESCI MISSION ALIGNMENT]
The Register Maxi project is a key enabler of the decentralized science (DeSci) movement, supporting its mission to democratize access to scientific knowledge, foster global collaboration, and ensure the transparency and integrity of scientific work. Here's how Register Maxi aligns with and supports the goals of DeSci:
 
 1. Enabling Transparency and Ownership in Scientific Research
 In the DeSci ecosystem, ensuring that scientific knowledge is accessible, verifiable, and owned by its creators is paramount. Register Maxi leverages blockchain technology to provide a secure and immutable record of intellectual property, including scientific research, innovations, and discoveries. By allowing researchers, scientists, and innovators to register their work on the blockchain, Register Maxi guarantees that the origin of their intellectual property is recorded transparently and permanently, reducing the risk of plagiarism, data manipulation, or misattribution of ideas. This directly supports DeSci? goal of fostering a more transparent and trustworthy scientific community.
 
 2. Promoting Decentralized Collaboration
 DeSci seeks to break down the traditional barriers in scientific research, enabling decentralized collaboration across borders and disciplines. Register Maxi contributes to this vision by offering a platform where scientists and creators can securely and efficiently document their work without relying on centralized institutions. The decentralized nature of Register Maxi aligns perfectly with DeSci? ethos of collaboration and democratization of scientific knowledge. By enabling the easy registration and verification of innovations, the platform helps connect researchers and creators from around the world, promoting cross-border collaboration and the free exchange of ideas.
 
 3. Protecting Intellectual Property in a Digital World
 The decentralized nature of DeSci promotes open access to scientific data, but it also raises concerns about intellectual property protection, especially in a digital landscape. Register Maxi provides a solution by offering a secure, blockchain-based platform for intellectual property protection, ensuring that creators retain control over their work while contributing to the open scientific ecosystem. The platform offers a legally valid, low-cost alternative to traditional intellectual property registration methods, allowing innovators to safeguard their contributions without the delays and complexities associated with conventional systems. This aligns with DeSci? mission to empower creators and ensure that their work is properly recognized and protected.
 
 4. Enhancing the Efficiency of Scientific Discovery
 One of DeSci? goals is to streamline and enhance the efficiency of scientific research by leveraging cutting-edge technologies. Register Maxi supports this by enabling fast, efficient, and verifiable registration of research findings and innovations. The platform's quick, blockchain-enabled registration process (typically completed within minutes) contrasts with the slow and bureaucratic processes of traditional registration methods, helping researchers focus more on innovation and less on administrative tasks. By reducing the time and cost involved in protecting intellectual property, Register Maxi accelerates the pace of scientific discovery, helping drive forward DeSci's vision of faster, more accessible innovation.
 
 5. Supporting the Global DeSci Ecosystem
 The global nature of the DeSci movement calls for tools that can scale and support cross-border initiatives. Register Maxi is designed to be globally recognized, with its blockchain-based registration process validated under international agreements like the Berne Convention. This makes it a valuable tool for the global DeSci community, ensuring that innovations from any corner of the world can be securely protected and shared across the ecosystem. By supporting the globalization of decentralized science, Register Maxi strengthens the overall DeSci network and helps foster an inclusive scientific community.
 
 6. Creating a Framework for Fair Monetization of Scientific Work
 DeSci aims to create a new framework where researchers and innovators can directly monetize their work, removing intermediaries and empowering creators. Register Maxi supports this by providing a transparent record of ownership that can be leveraged in licensing agreements, patents, and intellectual property deals. This direct control over intellectual property allows DeSci participants to monetize their contributions fairly and maintain control over how their work is used, in line with DeSci? goal of empowering scientists and creators to benefit from their innovations.
 
 The Register Maxi supports the DeSci movement by providing a secure, decentralized platform for intellectual property protection, fostering collaboration, ensuring transparency, and enabling efficient scientific discovery. Through its blockchain-based approach, Register Maxi helps build a more open, equitable, and innovative scientific ecosystem?ore principles of DeSci?y empowering creators with the tools needed to protect, share, and monetize their work in a decentralized world.

[USE OF FUNDS]
The funds from the grants program for the Register Maxi project will be strategically allocated to support the development and expansion of version 2.0 of the platform, incorporating new features, a revised front-end, and several other key enhancements outlined in the roadmap. These funds will be crucial in advancing the platform's capabilities, improving user experience, and ensuring its scalability for a broader audience. Here's how the funds will be utilized:
 
 1. Development of New Features (Version 2.0)
 The next phase of Register Maxi will focus on the introduction of several critical new features designed to enhance the platform? functionality and security. The funds will be used to:
 
 Integrate advanced blockchain functionalities such as smart contract capabilities for managing intellectual property (IP) rights, automating the licensing process, and ensuring transparency in content creation and ownership.
 
 Support integration with decentralized identity (DID) systems, enabling users to verify their identity in a secure and privacy-preserving manner, a key requirement for the global scalability of the platform.
 
 Enhance metadata encryption to provide more robust privacy protection for users while ensuring that their intellectual property remains verifiable and accessible on the blockchain.
 
 2. Improved Front-End Design and User Experience
 One of the core focuses of the grant funds will be on improving the platform's front-end to ensure a seamless, intuitive, and accessible experience for all users, from individual creators to large organizations. This includes:
 
 Redesigning the user interface (UI) to make it more user-friendly, visually appealing, and easier to navigate, ensuring that even users with minimal technical knowledge can interact with the platform with ease.
 
 Mobile optimization to provide users with a fully responsive and functional experience across devices, enabling creators to manage their intellectual property securely from smartphones and tablets.
 
 Simplified registration processes to make the creation, registration, and verification of digital files as quick and straightforward as possible, reducing the barriers to entry for new users.
 
 3. Scalability and Infrastructure Enhancements
 As the platform scales to meet the demands of a growing global user base, significant investments will be made in improving the infrastructure:
 
 Server infrastructure upgrades to handle increased traffic and provide a faster, more reliable service as more users and transactions are added.
 
 Blockchain network optimization to ensure that the platform remains fast, secure, and cost-effective as it expands its user base and integrates more features.
 
 Decentralized storage solutions to ensure that the platform can handle large volumes of data while maintaining the integrity and security of the files registered on the blockchain.
 
 4. Global Expansion and Localization
 The funds will also be directed towards expanding the platform's global reach:
 
 Localization efforts to provide multi-language support, enabling users from different regions to access the platform in their native languages.
 
 Regional partnerships and outreach programs to onboard new users from key markets and raise awareness of the platform? benefits for intellectual property protection.
 
 Legal and regulatory compliance to ensure that Register Maxi meets local laws and standards in different countries, making it easier for users to trust and adopt the platform on a global scale.
 
 5. Research and Development (R&D) for New Technologies
 Funds will be used for ongoing research and development to explore cutting-edge technologies that can further enhance the platform. This could include:
 
 Integration with emerging decentralized technologies, such as Web3 and decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs), which can help expand the platform? functionality and governance capabilities.
 
 AI-powered tools for automatically identifying, categorizing, and verifying intellectual property submissions, enhancing efficiency and reducing the chances of human error in the registration process.
 
 6. Community Building and Ecosystem Support
 The grant funds will also support the growth and engagement of the Register Maxi user community:
 
 Educational initiatives to help users understand the value of decentralized intellectual property protection and how to use the platform effectively.
 
 Community-driven features where users can submit suggestions for new features, participate in decision-making processes, and contribute to the platform's ongoing evolution.
 
 Incentive programs to encourage active participation and growth within the community, including rewarding users who contribute valuable content, offer feedback, or help onboard new users.
 
 7. Marketing and Awareness Campaigns
 To ensure that Register Maxi version 2.0 reaches a wider audience, part of the funds will be allocated to:
 
 Marketing campaigns to raise awareness of the platform's capabilities, targeting specific user groups such as creators, researchers, innovators, and businesses.
 
 Partnerships with organizations in the intellectual property, innovation, and academic sectors to promote the benefits of Register Maxi and encourage adoption of the platform.

[PAST ROUND WALLET]
Yes, GG21 round.

[NEW UPDATES]
Since participating in previous DeSci grant rounds, the resources allocated have been used to significantly enhance the registration and validation processes of the Register Maxi platform. These funds allowed for the real-world validation of the platform with a live client, ensuring that the system operates as intended and can handle real-world use cases effectively. Additionally, the grant supported the costs of conducting essential testing to fine-tune the platform? functionalities and user experience.
 
 While the resources from the previous round were instrumental in improving the initial version of the platform, they were relatively modest and focused on foundational enhancements. Moving forward, the funds from this round will allow for further refinements and the implementation of more advanced features, particularly those outlined in Register Maxi version 2.0. These improvements will include smart contract integrations, decentralized identity verification, and global localization efforts to expand the platform? accessibility and capabilities for a broader, international user base.
 
 The current round of funding will thus play a critical role in advancing the platform to the next stage of its development, supporting the scalability and global adoption of the project.

[OTHER INFORMATION]
We are excited about the potential of the Register Maxi project and its alignment with the DeSci movement, which is revolutionizing the way scientific research, intellectual property, and innovation are approached globally. Beyond the technical developments and milestones we've achieved, we would like to highlight a few key aspects that we believe set Register Maxi apart and add value to the DeSci ecosystem:
 
 Previous Work and Expertise
 Our team has extensive experience in the fields of blockchain development, intellectual property management, and decentralized applications. Over the years, we have successfully built and launched platforms that integrate emerging technologies such as blockchain, AI, and smart contracts to address real-world problems. This expertise directly informs the development of Register Maxi, ensuring that it is not only innovative but also practical, secure, and user-centric.
 
 Additionally, our collaboration with deScier has allowed us to build a strong network within the DeSci community, which gives us access to a diverse pool of researchers, innovators, and thought leaders. This partnership is key in ensuring that Register Maxi remains at the forefront of the DeSci movement, facilitating collaboration, knowledge sharing, and the decentralization of scientific work.
 
 Other Project Affiliations
 We have been involved in several other initiatives that align with DeSci principles, including the development of decentralized platforms for research data sharing and decentralized education portals for upskilling scientists in emerging technologies. Our involvement in these projects further strengthens our understanding of the needs and challenges within the decentralized science ecosystem.
 
 Our work with other DeSci projects is complementary, as we are committed to helping grow the decentralized science ecosystem, providing valuable tools for researchers, and creating an environment where intellectual property is protected and accessible to all. Register Maxi? capabilities seamlessly integrate with other decentralized platforms, making it a valuable asset for broader DeSci initiatives.
 
 Potential Conflicts of Interest
 We are committed to ensuring that Register Maxi is developed with integrity, transparency, and full alignment with the principles of DeSci. At this time, there are no potential conflicts of interest that would hinder the development or operation of the project. Our sole focus is to create a platform that serves the needs of the DeSci community and fosters a decentralized and equitable future for scientific research and intellectual property management.
 
 Relevance to the DeSci Movement
 As we look ahead, we believe that Register Maxi can play a pivotal role in supporting DeSci's mission by providing a reliable, secure, and user-friendly platform for researchers and innovators worldwide. By helping users register and protect their intellectual property in a decentralized and transparent manner, we are furthering the principles of open access, collaboration, and democratization of science that are foundational to the DeSci ecosystem.
 
 In conclusion, Register Maxi is not just a platform for intellectual property registration; it is a comprehensive solution that enables the growth, protection, and fair monetization of decentralized scientific work. With the support of the DeSci community and the DeSci grant program, we are confident that we can take the project to new heights and contribute meaningfully to the global movement of decentralized science.
